rationale_overall: Watchlist [NVDA, URA, RKLB, XLE, LNG] is at cap=5 and every slot has a fresh, dated Q1 2023 tailwind, so the displacement bar is high. (1) NVDA: Microsoft's $10B OpenAI investment (2023-01-23), GPT-4 launch (2023-03-14), and Nvidia GTC cuLitho announcement (2023-03-21) all land inside the window and reinforce the AI flagship. (2) URA: Senate/House bills to cut off Russian low-enriched uranium imports were advancing in Q1 2023 with bipartisan and DOE support; term uranium pricing strengthened as utilities returned to the market. (3) RKLB: completed its first U.S. launch from LC-2 Virginia in January 2023 and ran simultaneous Electron campaigns across NZ and VA in March 2023; Neutron development hit Archimedes igniter and stage-two-tank milestones. (4) LNG: Cheniere exported record Q1 2023 LNG cargos to Europe (>70% of output going to Europe), the cleanest single-stock expression of the geopolitical-energy wave with a fresh dated catalyst. (5) XLE: broad energy sector ETF still benefiting from sustained Russia-Ukraine supply disruption and OPEC+ discipline; the second leg of the geopolitical-energy double-stack the profile's non-tech bucket explicitly contemplates. The most plausible add would be TSLA on Tesla Investor Day Optimus humanoid showcase (2023-03-01/02), which is genuinely fresh and the profile names robotics as a next-wave. But at cap=5 funding it would require dropping either XLE (collapsing geopolitical-energy from 2 to 1 slots) or another holding with an equally fresh dated catalyst. A demo-video humanoid vision does not clear that bar against four named waves all carrying live Q1 2023 commercial catalysts; this is exactly the marginal-churn trade the prior quarter's no_changes rationale flagged. Quantum (IONQ removed 2022-Q3) and demographics still lack a fresh dated Q1 2023 trigger. All catalysts verified via WebSearch before:2023-04-01; no post-date events referenced (Nvidia Q2 2023-05-24, GB200 2024-03-18, etc. all suppressed).
